steps.title: Spot the clause types
steps.content:
Left of the blank: “That the geographic center of North America lay in the state of North Dakota was conceded by all” is a clause that can stand on its own (the ‘That…’ part serves as the subject).
Right of the blank: “establishing its precise coordinates proved more divisive” is also a clause that can stand on its own (the gerund phrase 'establishing …' serves as the subject).

steps.title: Pick the punctuation
steps.content:
A semicolon separates two independent clauses, so choice C (“involved;”) correctly joins the clauses.
Choices A, B, and
  • D each fail: A lacks punctuation creating a run‑on
  • B uses a colon which requires an explanation or list
  • D uses only a comma causing a comma splice
.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
